seoTitle: How to Log In to Cloudflare (2026)
metaDescription: Sign in to your Cloudflare dashboard using email login or SSO, pass the human verification challenge, and complete two-factor authentication.
intro: Logging into Cloudflare involves more than just a password these days, with bot-detection checks and two-factor authentication as standard steps. This guide walks through the full sign-in flow, including alternate login methods.

steps.heading: Go to the Cloudflare login page
steps.body: Open your browser and navigate to the main Cloudflare dashboard login screen.

steps.heading: Enter your credentials
steps.body: Type your registered email address and password into the login form.

steps.heading: Try alternate sign-in options if needed
steps.body: Use Google, GitHub, or your organization's Single Sign-On path instead of email and password if that's how your account is set up.

steps.heading: Pass the human verification challenge
steps.body: Complete the Turnstile 'Verify you are human' check that appears during login.

steps.heading: Enter your two-factor code
steps.body: Input your mobile authentication passcode or use a security key, then click Sign In to reach your dashboard.

faq.question: What do I do if I forgot my Cloudflare password?
faq.answer: Use the password reset link on the login page to receive a reset email and create a new password.

faq.question: Can I log in with Google or GitHub instead of a password?
faq.answer: Yes, Cloudflare supports social sign-in options like Google and GitHub, as well as corporate SSO for eligible accounts.

faq.question: Why am I stuck on the verification challenge during login?
faq.answer: This is usually caused by browser cookies, extensions, or network issues; try clearing cookies or using a clean browser session.

faq.question: Is two-factor authentication required to log in?
faq.answer: If you've enabled 2FA on your account, you'll need to enter your authentication code or use a hardware key every time you sign in.
